{"query":"Goldman Sachs Devin software engineer Employee #1 deployment","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1660,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.ibm.com/think/news/goldman-sachs-first-ai-employee-devin","title":"Meet Devin the AI Software Engineer, Employee #1 in Goldman Sachs’ “Hybrid Workforce” | IBM","content":"Goldman Sachs has just deployed “Devin,” a generative AI (gen AI)-based full-stack developer, Goldman CIO Marco Argenti told CNBC in a July 11 interview.
And while the banking world has been using AI-related tech for decades, Devin is the “ first AI software engineer,” according to its creator Cognition.
Shanker Ramamurthy, Managing Partner of Global Banking & Financial Markets at IBM, offered an example of how Devin could be helpful to an institution like Goldman.
“If Devin increases productivity by 20%, then Goldman’s 12,000 technologists can now operate like 14,400 technologists.”
Devin is just the latest in a long line of AI-based coding tools the global investment bank has used, including the much-lauded GitHub Copilot(not to be confused with Microsoft’s AI tool), which OpenAI and GitHub released in 2021.
According to Cognition’s website, the company designed Devin to be a “tireless, skilled teammate,” with the ability to create an app from soup to nuts."},{"url":"https://fortune.com/2025/07/14/goldman-sachs-ai-powered-software-engineer-devin-new-employee-increase-productivity-fears-of-job-replacement/","title":"Goldman Sachs doesn't have to hire a $180,000 software engineer—meet Devin, its new AI-powered worker | Fortune","content":"Goldman Sachs doesn’t have to hire a $180,000 software engineer—meet Devin, its new AI-powered worker
Goldman Sachs just hired Devin, an AI-powered software engineer that’s capable of coding just as well as humans—minus six-figure salaries.
While the firm’s tech leader sees a future that’s a “hybrid workforce” between humans and AI, leaders like Ford CEO Jim Farley warn of a decrease in white-collar work.
Rather, it will be working all day on its coding assignments.
The Wall Street investment bank recently unveiled it had hired Devin: a new AI-powered autonomous software engineer, created by AI startup Cognition."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2025/07/11/goldman-sachs-autonomous-coder-pilot-marks-major-ai-milestone.html","title":"Goldman Sachs autonomous coder pilot marks major AI milestone","content":"The bank is testing an autonomous software engineer from artificial intelligence startup Cognition that is expected to soon join the ranks of the firm's 12,000 human developers, Goldman tech chief Marco Argenti told CNBC.
Just last year, Wall Street firms including JPMorgan Chase and Morgan Stanley were rolling out cognitive assistants based on OpenAI models to get employees acquainted with the technology.
Goldman is the first major bank to use Devin, according to Cognition, which was founded in late 2023 by a trio of engineers and whose staff is reportedly stocked with champion coders.
Executives at companies from Amazon to Ford have grown more candid about what AI will mean for hiring plans."},{"url":"https://www.hcamag.com/us/specialization/hr-technology/new-employee-goldman-sachs-deploys-ai-software-engineer/542377","title":"'New employee': Goldman Sachs deploys AI software engineer | Human Resources Director","content":"Marco Argenti, chief information officer of Goldman Sachs, said the company will introduce to their workforce the programme, named Devin, which has been dubbed as the "first AI software engineer."
However, it will remain supervised by the company’s human employees.
Argenti said Devin's deployment at Goldman Sachs may scale into thousands."},{"url":"https://www.hrkatha.com/news/goldman-sachs-deploys-ai-software-engineer-devin-to-transform-tech-roles/","title":"Goldman Sachs deploys AI software engineer Devin to transform tech roles","content":"The bank plans to introduce hundreds of these AI coders initially, with the intention to scale the deployment to thousands."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Goldman Sachs Devin software engineer Employee #1 deployment","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.ibm.com/think/news/goldman-sachs-first-ai-employee-devin","title":"Meet Devin the AI Software Engineer, Employee #1 in ...","content":"Goldman Sachs has just deployed “Devin,” a generative AI (gen AI)-based full-stack developer, Goldman CIO Marco Argenti told CNBC in a July 11 interview. “Devin is going to be like our new employee,” said Argenti, “who’s going to start doing stuff on the behalf of our developers.” Devin can execute entire apps from scratch based on natural-language verbal prompts. And while the banking world has been using AI-related tech for decades, Devin is the “first AI software engineer,” according to its creator Cognition. Devin is part of Argenti’s vision for a “hybrid workforce” where AI agents work alongside their flesh-and-blood colleagues. If you’re a commodities trader, you can ask Devin, ‘Hey, help me incorporate this new information into my model.’” Given access to relevant data and models, Devin could rapidly work with a technologist to co-create or refine and help implement an application to process the request, streamlining and accelerating the software development life cycle.","score":0.86052185,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.hcamag.com/us/specialization/hr-technology/new-employee-goldman-sachs-deploys-ai-software-engineer/542377","title":"'New employee': Goldman Sachs deploys AI software engineer","content":"Devin, which has been dubbed as the \"first AI software engineer.\" Devin will handle work such as updating internal code to newer programming","score":0.7576693,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.hrkatha.com/news/goldman-sachs-deploys-ai-software-engineer-devin-to-transform-tech-roles","title":"Goldman Sachs deploys AI software engineer Devin to transform tech roles","content":"Devin is designed to perform repetitive and time-consuming tasks such as updating legacy systems and automating basic software functions.","score":0.66777676,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://fortune.com/2025/07/14/goldman-sachs-ai-powered-software-engineer-devin-new-employee-increase-productivity-fears-of-job-replacement","title":"Goldman Sachs doesn't have to hire a $180,000 software engineer—meet Devin, its new AI-powered worker | Fortune","content":"# Goldman Sachs doesn’t have to hire a $180,000 software engineer—meet Devin, its new AI-powered worker. With the tool being capable of conducting end-to-end coding tasks, Goldman hopes it will improve worker productivity by up to three or four times the rate of previous AI tools, according to Goldman’s chief information officer Marco Argenti. Goldman Sachs plans to launch Devin by the hundreds—maybe eventually even by the thousands, Argenti added—joining the nearly 12,000 existing software engineers at the company. “Engineers are going to be expected to have the ability to really describe problems in a coherent way and turn it into prompts…and then be able to supervise the work of those agents.”. For the banking industry in particular, this AI-driven workforce transformation could lead to 200,000 fewer people on Wall Street within the next three to five years, according to Bloomberg*.* However, Argenti said that those who choose to embrace AI will be best equipped for a successful future, and it’s especially important for young talent facing dwindling entry-level roles.","score":0.523494,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.wilmott.com/goldman-sachs-deploys-ai-software-engineer-in-groundbreaking-pilot-program","title":"Goldman Sachs Deploys AI Software Engineer in Groundbreaking Pilot ...","content":"Goldman Sachs has become the first major bank to integrate an autonomous AI software engineer into its development operations, launching a pilot program","score":0.45231876,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.71,"request_id":"9f8c8ea1-9f9b-4339-ac4c-b054d6003061"}
